subject: Sociology
title: Spirituality Without Religion
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Spirituality Without Religion.

hook.text: I'm spiritual but not religious. It's the fastest-growing religious identity in the Western world.

buildup.text: The spiritual-but-not-religious reject institutions but seek meaning through meditation, nature, or practice.

discovery.text: This trend reflects individualism: people want to choose beliefs rather than inherit a preset package.

twist.text: Critics say pick-and-choose spirituality lacks the community and depth that institutions provide.

climax.text: The rise of spirituality without religion shows the need for meaning outlasts any one institution.

quiz.question: What drives the 'spiritual but not religious' trend?
options: Desire for personal choice over institutional religion
